_fixture_note: Real dry-run output from 2026-07-16 against two real, deliberately-built Snowflake schemas (DDL_TEST_SOURCE, DDL_TEST_TARGET) covering all 4 real scenarios at once: a missing table (PRODUCTS), a missing column (CUSTOMERS.LOYALTY_TIER), a type mismatch (ORDERS.AMOUNT), and an extra table in the target (LEGACY_AUDIT_LOG). This is captured AFTER a real bug found on the first --execute run: the DDL was originally unqualified (CREATE TABLE "PRODUCTS") and silently created the table in the connection's default schema instead of the real target -- fixed to always schema-qualify with the actual target schema name, visible in the planned_ddl below.

Column 'ORDERS.AMOUNT' is ('NUMBER', None, 10, 2) in source but ('NUMBER', None, 8, 0) in target -- type changes are never auto-generated (can be lossy/unsafe); resolve manually.
